import Foundation
import AppKit
import plistlib
from rotato.io import read_plist, write_plist
from rotato.archive import KeyedArchive

def make_attributed_string(text, font_size, font_weight):
    """Create an NSMutableAttributedString with just font attrs (color comes from SceneLabel)."""
    astr = Foundation.NSMutableAttributedString.alloc().initWithString_(text)
    font = AppKit.NSFont.systemFontOfSize_weight_(font_size, font_weight)
    attrs = {
        AppKit.NSFontAttributeName: font,
    }
    astr.setAttributes_range_(attrs, Foundation.NSMakeRange(0, astr.length()))
    archiver = Foundation.NSKeyedArchiver.alloc().initRequiringSecureCoding_(False)
    archiver.encodeObject_forKey_(astr, 'root')
    archiver.finishEncoding()
    return plistlib.loads(bytes(archiver.encodedData()))

def graft_attributed_string(target_objects, source_plist):
    src_objects = source_plist['$objects']
    root_uid = source_plist['$top']['root']
    base_offset = len(target_objects)
    
    def remap(val):
        if isinstance(val, plistlib.UID):
            return plistlib.UID(0) if val.data == 0 else plistlib.UID(val.data + base_offset - 1)
        elif isinstance(val, dict):
            return {k: remap(v) for k, v in val.items()}
        elif isinstance(val, list):
            return [remap(v) for v in val]
        return val
    
    for i in range(1, len(src_objects)):
        target_objects.append(remap(src_objects[i]))
    
    return plistlib.UID(root_uid.data + base_offset - 1)

# Rebuild from template to get clean state
from rotato.io import read_plist as rp
template = rp('templates/iphone14pro.rotato')

# But we want to keep our current file's settings, so load it
data = read_plist('/tmp/drift-directions-promo.rotato')
objects = data['$objects']
archive = KeyedArchive(data)
root = archive.root

label_store = archive.resolve(root['sceneLabelStore'])
all_dict = archive.resolve(label_store['all'])
keys = [archive.resolve(k) for k in all_dict['NS.keys']]

# Create attributed strings with reasonable font sizes
headline_plist = make_attributed_string('Navigate Your World', 36, AppKit.NSFontWeightBold)
subtitle_plist = make_attributed_string('AI-powered directions that actually make sense', 18, AppKit.NSFontWeightMedium)

# Graft and assign
headline_uid = graft_attributed_string(objects, headline_plist)
subtitle_uid = graft_attributed_string(objects, subtitle_plist)

for i, k in enumerate(keys):
    label = archive.resolve(all_dict['NS.objects'][i])
    
    # Fix ALL label colors to white (proper NSColor format)
    color = archive.resolve(label['color'])
    color['NSColorSpace'] = 3
    color['NSWhite'] = b'1\x00'
    color['NSComponents'] = b'1 1'
    # Restore custom color space from the original template if needed
    
    if k == 'sceneLabelTop':
        label['text'] = headline_uid
        # Move position higher above device
        pos = archive.resolve(label['position'])
        pos['NS.rectval'] = '{{0, 0.14}, {0, 0}}'
        print(f'Set headline at sceneLabelTop, pos y=0.14')
        
    elif k == 'sceneLabelBottom':
        label['text'] = subtitle_uid
        # Move position lower below device
        pos = archive.resolve(label['position'])
        pos['NS.rectval'] = '{{0, -0.14}, {0, 0}}'
        print(f'Set subtitle at sceneLabelBottom, pos y=-0.14')
    else:
        # Clear all other labels
        text_obj = archive.resolve(label['text'])
        nsstring = archive.resolve(text_obj['NSString']) if 'NSString' in text_obj else text_obj
        if isinstance(nsstring, dict) and 'NS.string' in nsstring:
            nsstring['NS.string'] = ''

# Set label store properties
label_store['scale'] = 50.0       # Smaller scale
label_store['levitation'] = 0.12  # Push text well in front of device
label_store['angle'] = 0

print(f'Scale: {label_store["scale"]}, Levitation: {label_store["levitation"]}')

write_plist(data, '/tmp/drift-directions-promo.rotato')
print('Saved!')
PYEOF

```bash
import Foundation
import AppKit
import plistlib

from rotato.archive import KeyedArchive

def make_attributed_string(text, font_size, font_weight):

    astr = Foundation.NSMutableAttributedString.alloc().initWithString_(text)

    attrs = {
        AppKit.NSFontAttributeName: font,
    }
    astr.setAttributes_range_(attrs, Foundation.NSMakeRange(0, astr.length()))
    archiver = Foundation.NSKeyedArchiver.alloc().initRequiringSecureCoding_(False)
    archiver.encodeObject_forKey_(astr, 'root')
    archiver.finishEncoding()
    return plistlib.loads(bytes(archiver.encodedData()))

def graft_attributed_string(target_objects, source_plist):
    src_objects = source_plist['$objects']
    root_uid = source_plist['$top']['root']
    base_offset = len(target_objects)
    
    def remap(val):
        if isinstance(val, plistlib.UID):
            return plistlib.UID(0) if val.data == 0 else plistlib.UID(val.data + base_offset - 1)
        elif isinstance(val, dict):
            return {k: remap(v) for k, v in val.items()}
        elif isinstance(val, list):
            return [remap(v) for v in val]
        return val
    
    for i in range(1, len(src_objects)):
        target_objects.append(remap(src_objects[i]))
    
    return plistlib.UID(root_uid.data + base_offset - 1)

subtitle_plist = make_attributed_string('AI-powered directions that actually make sense', 18, AppKit.NSFontWeightMedium)

        # Move position higher above device

        pos['NS.rectval'] = '{{0, 0.14}, {0, 0}}'

# Set label store properties

label_store['angle'] = 0
```

from rotato.io import read_plist
from rotato.archive import KeyedArchive
import plistlib

data = read_plist('/tmp/drift-directions-promo.rotato')
archive = KeyedArchive(data)
root = archive.root

# Label store top-level
label_store = archive.resolve(root['sceneLabelStore'])
print('=== LABEL STORE ===')
for k, v in label_store.items():
    if k == '$class': continue
    r = archive.resolve(v) if isinstance(v, plistlib.UID) else v
    if isinstance(r, dict):
        cls = archive.get_class_name(r)
        if cls == 'NSDictionary':
            continue  # skip 'all' dict
        print(f'  {k}: <{cls}>')
    elif r is None:
        print(f'  {k}: None')
    else:
        print(f'  {k}: {r!r}')

# All labels
all_dict = archive.resolve(label_store['all'])
keys = [archive.resolve(k) for k in all_dict['NS.keys']]
objs = all_dict['NS.objects']

print('\n=== ALL LABELS ===')
for i, k in enumerate(keys):
    label = archive.resolve(objs[i])
    text_obj = archive.resolve(label['text'])
    
    # Get text string
    nsstring_obj = archive.resolve(text_obj.get('NSString', plistlib.UID(0)))
    text_str = ''
    if isinstance(nsstring_obj, dict):
        text_str = nsstring_obj.get('NS.string', '')
    
    if not text_str:
        continue  # skip empty labels
    
    print(f'\n--- {k} ---')
    print(f'  text: {text_str!r}')
    
    # Position
    pos = archive.resolve(label['position'])
    if isinstance(pos, dict) and 'NS.rectval' in pos:
        rv = archive.resolve(pos['NS.rectval'])
        print(f'  position: {rv}')
    
    # Color
    color = archive.resolve(label['color'])
    print(f'  color:')
    for ck, cv in color.items():
        if ck == '$class': continue
        cr = archive.resolve(cv) if isinstance(cv, plistlib.UID) else cv
        if isinstance(cr, bytes) and len(cr) < 100:
            print(f'    {ck}: {cr!r}')
        elif isinstance(cr, dict):
            print(f'    {ck}: <{archive.get_class_name(cr)}>')
        else:
            print(f'    {ck}: {cr!r}')
    
    # Attributed string details
    print(f'  attributed string keys: {[x for x in text_obj.keys() if x != "$class"]}')
    if 'NSAttributes' in text_obj:
        attrs = archive.resolve(text_obj['NSAttributes'])
        if isinstance(attrs, dict):
            attr_keys = attrs.get('NS.keys', [])
            attr_objs = attrs.get('NS.objects', [])
            rkeys = [archive.resolve(ak) for ak in attr_keys] if attr_keys else []
            print(f'  attr keys: {rkeys}')
            for j, rk in enumerate(rkeys):
                attr_val = archive.resolve(attr_objs[j])
                if isinstance(attr_val, dict):
                    cls = archive.get_class_name(attr_val)
                    print(f'    {rk}: <{cls}>')
                    for ak2, av2 in attr_val.items():
                        if ak2 == '$class': continue
                        ar2 = archive.resolve(av2) if isinstance(av2, plistlib.UID) else av2
                        if isinstance(ar2, bytes) and len(ar2) > 200:
                            print(f'      {ak2}: <bytes {len(ar2)}>')
                        elif isinstance(ar2, dict):
                            print(f'      {ak2}: <{archive.get_class_name(ar2)}>')
                        else:
                            print(f'      {ak2}: {ar2!r}')
                else:
                    print(f'    {rk}: {attr_val!r}')
    
    if 'NSAttributeInfo' in text_obj:
        info = archive.resolve(text_obj['NSAttributeInfo'])
        print(f'  NSAttributeInfo: <bytes {len(info)}>' if isinstance(info, bytes) else f'  NSAttributeInfo: {info!r}')

<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: cd /Users/andycross/video-gen && uv run python << 'PYEOF'
import Foundation
import AppKit
import plistlib
from rotato.io import read_plist, write_plist
from rotato.archive import KeyedArchive

def make_attributed_string(text, font_name, font_size):
    """Create an NSMutableAttributedString with a named font."""
    astr = Foundation.NSMutableAttributedString.alloc().initWithString_(text)
    font = AppKit.NSFont.fontWithName_size_(font_name, font_size)
    if font is None:
        raise ValueError(f"Font '{font_name}' not found")
    attrs = { AppKit.NSFontAttributeName: font }
    astr.setAttributes_range_(attrs, Foundation.NSMakeRange(0, astr.length()))
    
    archiver = Foundation.NSKeyedArchiver.alloc().initRequiringSecureCoding_(False)
    archiver.encodeObject_forKey_(astr, 'root')
    archiver.finishEncoding()
    return plistlib.loads(bytes(archiver.encodedData()))

def graft_attributed_string(target_objects, source_plist):
    src_objects = source_plist['$objects']
    root_uid = source_plist['$top']['root']
    base_offset = len(target_objects)
    
    def remap(val):
        if isinstance(val, plistlib.UID):
            return plistlib.UID(0) if val.data == 0 else plistlib.UID(val.data + base_offset - 1)
        elif isinstance(val, dict):
            return {k: remap(v) for k, v in val.items()}
        elif isinstance(val, list):
            return [remap(v) for v in val]
        return val
    
    for i in range(1, len(src_objects)):
        target_objects.append(remap(src_objects[i]))
    
    return plistlib.UID(root_uid.data + base_offset - 1)

# Start fresh from the template
from shutil import copy2
copy2('templates/iphone14pro.rotato', '/tmp/drift-directions-promo.rotato')

# Re-apply our previous settings
from rotato import file as rf, modify
doc = rf.load('/tmp/drift-directions-promo.rotato')

# Screen image
data = read_plist('/tmp/drift-directions-promo.rotato')
archive = KeyedArchive(data)

# Inject screen image
screen_idx, screen_node = archive.find_node_by_name('Screen')
geom = archive.resolve(screen_node['geometry'])
mats = archive.resolve(geom['materials'])
mat = archive.resolve(mats['NS.objects'][0])
diffuse = archive.resolve(mat['diffuse'])
img_data = open('/tmp/directions_frame.png', 'rb').read()
objects = data['$objects']
new_idx = len(objects)
objects.append(img_data)
diffuse['contents'] = plistlib.UID(new_idx)

# Background gradient
root = archive.root
# We'll set bg after saving, for now focus on labels

# Label store settings (matching what Rotato actually uses)
label_store = archive.resolve(root['sceneLabelStore'])
label_store['scale'] = 1.0
label_store['levitation'] = 1.8  # Slightly more than user's 1.54
label_store['angle'] = 0
label_store['padding'] = 0.01

all_dict = archive.resolve(label_store['all'])
keys = [archive.resolve(k) for k in all_dict['NS.keys']]

# Create attributed strings with scene-scale font sizes
headline_plist = make_attributed_string('Navigate Your World', 'HelveticaNeue-Bold', 380)
subtitle_plist = make_attributed_string('AI-powered directions that actually make sense', 'HelveticaNeue', 200)

headline_uid = graft_attributed_string(objects, headline_plist)
subtitle_uid = graft_attributed_string(objects, subtitle_plist)

def set_color_white(color_obj):
    """Set a SceneLabel color to white using the format Rotato expects."""
    color_obj['NSColorSpace'] = 1
    color_obj['NSComponents'] = b'1 1 1 1'
    color_obj['NSRGB'] = b'1 1 1\x00'
    # Remove old greyscale keys if present
    for old_key in ['NSWhite', 'NSCustomColorSpace']:
        if old_key in color_obj:
            del color_obj[old_key]

for i, k in enumerate(keys):
    label = archive.resolve(all_dict['NS.objects'][i])
    color = archive.resolve(label['color'])
    
    if k == 'sceneLabelTop':
        label['text'] = headline_uid
        pos = archive.resolve(label['position'])
        pos['NS.rectval'] = '{{0, 0.035}, {0, 0}}'
        set_color_white(color)
        print(f'Set headline: sceneLabelTop')
        
    elif k == 'sceneLabelBottom':
        label['text'] = subtitle_uid
        pos = archive.resolve(label['position'])
        pos['NS.rectval'] = '{{0, -0.06}, {0, 0}}'
        set_color_white(color)
        print(f'Set subtitle: sceneLabelBottom')
    else:
        # Clear other labels
        text_obj = archive.resolve(label['text'])
        nsstring = archive.resolve(text_obj.get('NSString', plistlib.UID(0)))
        if isinstance(nsstring, dict) and 'NS.string' in nsstring:
            nsstring['NS.string'] = ''

write_plist(data, '/tmp/drift-directions-promo.rotato')

# Now apply bg/render/animation settings via MCP-style calls
data2 = read_plist('/tmp/drift-directions-promo.rotato')
archive2 = KeyedArchive(data2)
doc2 = rf.load('/tmp/drift-directions-promo.rotato')

modify.set_background_gradient(doc2, (0.059, 0.125, 0.153), (0.173, 0.325, 0.392), 'linear')
modify.set_duration(doc2, 6.0)
modify.set_render_config(doc2, width=1290, height=2796, fps=60)
modify.toggle_shadows(doc2, True)
modify.set_shadow_intensity(doc2, 0.6)
modify.set_field_of_view(doc2, 55)

rf.save(doc2, '/tmp/drift-directions-promo.rotato')
print('\nAll settings applied and saved!')
PYEOF

import shutil
# Copy to a new filename so Rotato opens it fresh
shutil.copy2('/tmp/drift-directions-promo.rotato', '/tmp/drift-promo-v2.rotato')

# Verify the values in the new copy
from rotato.io import read_plist
from rotato.archive import KeyedArchive
data = read_plist('/tmp/drift-promo-v2.rotato')
archive = KeyedArchive(data)
root = archive.root
ls = archive.resolve(root['sceneLabelStore'])
print(f'levitation: {ls["levitation"]} (type: {type(ls["levitation"]).__name__})')
print(f'scale: {ls["scale"]}')

all_dict = archive.resolve(ls['all'])
keys = [archive.resolve(k) for k in all_dict['NS.keys']]
for i, k in enumerate(keys):
    label = archive.resolve(all_dict['NS.objects'][i])
    text_obj = archive.resolve(label['text'])
    nsstring = archive.resolve(text_obj.get('NSString', None))
    if nsstring and isinstance(nsstring, dict):
        s = nsstring.get('NS.string', '')
        if s:
            # Check font
            attr_keys = []
            if 'NSAttributes' in text_obj:
                attrs = archive.resolve(text_obj['NSAttributes'])
                ak = [archive.resolve(x) for x in attrs.get('NS.keys', [])]
                for j, a in enumerate(ak):
                    av = archive.resolve(attrs['NS.objects'][j])
                    if isinstance(av, dict):

import liblzfse
import plistlib

# Read v4 (our plutil-serialized version)
raw_v4 = liblzfse.decompress(open('/tmp/drift-promo-v4.rotato', 'rb').read())

raw_v2 = liblzfse.decompress(open('/tmp/drift-promo-v2.rotato', 'rb').read())
data_v2 = plistlib.loads(raw_v2)

# Compare label stores byte-for-byte in the raw binary
# Find "levitation" in both raw binaries
def find_bytes(haystack, needle):
    positions = []
    start = 0
    while True:
        pos = haystack.find(needle, start)
        if pos == -1:
            break
        positions.append(pos)
        start = pos + 1
    return positions

lev_bytes = b'levitation'
v2_positions = find_bytes(raw_v2, lev_bytes)
v4_positions = find_bytes(raw_v4, lev_bytes)

print(f'v2 "levitation" at offsets: {v2_positions}')
print(f'v4 "levitation" at offsets: {v4_positions}')

# Show bytes around levitation in both
for label, raw, positions in [('v2', raw_v2, v2_positions), ('v4', raw_v4, v4_positions)]:
    for pos in positions:
        # Show 50 bytes before and 80 bytes after
        start = max(0, pos - 20)
        end = min(len(raw), pos + len(lev_bytes) + 50)
        chunk = raw[start:end]
        print(f'\n{label} context around offset {pos}:')
        print(f'  hex: {chunk.hex()}')
        # Show the 8 bytes after "levitation" (likely the float value)
        after_key = raw[pos + len(lev_bytes):pos + len(lev_bytes) + 20]
        print(f'  after "levitation": {after_key.hex()}')
        import struct
        # Try to find the float 3.0 nearby
        for offset in range(pos, min(pos + 100, len(raw) - 8)):
            try:
                val = struct.unpack('>d', raw[offset:offset+8])[0]
                if abs(val - 3.0) < 0.001:
                    print(f'  Found 3.0 at offset {offset} (relative +{offset-pos})')
                    break
            except:
                pass

# Also verify the actual plist data
objs_v4 = data_v4['$objects']
for i, obj in enumerate(objs_v4):
    if isinstance(obj, dict) and 'levitation' in obj:
        print(f'\nv4 label store [{i}]:')
        for k, v in obj.items():
            if k == '$class': continue
            print(f'  {k}: {v!r} ({type(v).__name__})')

# Compare the raw binary sizes around the label store
print(f'\nv2 raw size: {len(raw_v2)}')
print(f'v4 raw size: {len(raw_v4)}')

# Count byte differences
diffs = 0
for j in range(min(len(raw_v2), len(raw_v4))):
    if raw_v2[j] != raw_v4[j]:
        diffs += 1
print(f'Byte differences: {diffs}')

# Load Rotato-saved v5 (user just saved with levitation set)

# Also load the pre-save v2 that Rotato saved earlier (known working)
raw_v2 = liblzfse.decompress(open('/tmp/drift-promo-v2.rotato', 'rb').read())

def analyze_around_levitation(raw, label):
    pos = raw.find(b'levitation')
    if pos == -1:
        print(f'{label}: "levitation" not found!')
        return
    
    # Show 100 bytes before and 100 bytes after
    start = max(0, pos - 100)
    end = min(len(raw), pos + 200)
    
    print(f'\n=== {label} ===')
    print(f'"levitation" at offset {pos}')
    
    # Hex dump with offset markers
    chunk = raw[start:end]
    for row_start in range(0, len(chunk), 32):
        row = chunk[row_start:row_start+32]
        offset = start + row_start
        hex_str = ' '.join(f'{b:02x}' for b in row)
        ascii_str = ''.join(chr(b) if 32 <= b < 127 else '.' for b in row)

```bash
cd /Users/andycross/video-gen && uv run python << 'PYEOF'
import liblzfse
import struct

# Load Rotato-saved v5 (user just saved with levitation set)
raw_rotato = liblzfse.decompress(open('/tmp/drift-promo-v5.rotato', 'rb').read())

# Also load the pre-save v2 that Rotato saved earlier (known working)
raw_v2 = liblzfse.decompress(open('/tmp/drift-promo-v2.rotato', 'rb').read())

def analyze_around_levitation(raw, label):
    pos = raw.find(b'levitation')
    if pos == -1:
        print(f'{label}: "levitation" not found!')
        return
    
    # Show 100 bytes before and 100 bytes after
    start = max(0, pos - 100)
    end = min(len(raw), pos + 200)
    
    print(f'\n=== {label} ===')
    print(f'"levitation" at offset {pos}')
    
    # Hex dump with offset markers
    chunk = raw[start:end]
    for row_start in range(0, len(chunk), 32):
        row = chunk[row_start:row_start+32]
        offset = start + row_start
        hex_str = ' '.join(f'{b:02x}' for b in row)
        ascii_str = ''.join(chr(b) if 32 <= b < 127 else '.' for b in row)
        marker = ' <-- levitation' if (pos - start) >= row_start and (pos - start) < row_start + 32 else ''
        print(f'  {offset:7d}: {hex_str}  {ascii_str}{marker}')

analyze_around_levitation(raw_v2, 'v2 (Rotato original)')
analyze_around_levitation(raw_rotato, 'v5 (Rotato re-saved)')

# Now let me create what OUR pipeline would produce from v2
import plistlib, subprocess, tempfile
from pathlib import Path

data = plistlib.loads(raw_v2)
# Don't modify anything - just round-trip
with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(suffix='.plist', delete=False) as tmp:
    tmp_path = tmp.name
    plistlib.dump(data, tmp, fmt=plistlib.FMT_BINARY)
subprocess.run(['plutil', '-convert', 'xml1', tmp_path], check=True, capture_output=True)
subprocess.run(['plutil', '-convert', 'binary1', tmp_path], check=True, capture_output=True)
raw_ours = Path(tmp_path).read_bytes()
Path(tmp_path).unlink()

analyze_around_levitation(raw_ours, 'OUR pipeline (no changes)')

# Also try: just plistlib binary, no plutil
raw_plistlib = plistlib.dumps(data, fmt=plistlib.FMT_BINARY)
analyze_around_levitation(raw_plistlib, 'plistlib only (no plutil)')
```

import liblzfse, plistlib, subprocess, tempfile
from pathlib import Path

# Take v2 (Rotato-saved), write with plistlib, convert with plutil, check XML
raw_v2 = liblzfse.decompress(open('/tmp/drift-promo-v2.rotato', 'rb').read())
data = plistlib.loads(raw_v2)

# Write binary with plistlib
tmp_bin = tempfile.mktemp(suffix='.plist')
with open(tmp_bin, 'wb') as f:
    plistlib.dump(data, f, fmt=plistlib.FMT_BINARY)

# Convert to XML with plutil
tmp_xml = tmp_bin + '.xml'
import shutil
shutil.copy(tmp_bin, tmp_xml)
subprocess.run(['plutil', '-convert', 'xml1', tmp_xml], check=True, capture_output=True)

# Read the XML and find the levitation region
xml = open(tmp_xml).read()

# Find levitation in XML

if idx >= 0:
    print('=== XML around "levitation" ===')
    # Show surrounding context
    start = max(0, idx - 200)
    end = min(len(xml), idx + 300)
    print(xml[start:end])

# Also check: what does plutil do with the ORIGINAL Rotato binary?
tmp_orig = tempfile.mktemp(suffix='.plist')
with open(tmp_orig, 'wb') as f:
    f.write(raw_v2)
subprocess.run(['plutil', '-convert', 'xml1', tmp_orig], check=True, capture_output=True)
xml_orig = open(tmp_orig).read()
idx_orig = xml_orig.find('levitation')
if idx_orig >= 0:
    print('\n=== ORIGINAL Rotato binary -> XML around "levitation" ===')
    start = max(0, idx_orig - 200)
    end = min(len(xml_orig), idx_orig + 300)
    print(xml_orig[start:end])

# Clean up

    Path(f).unlink(missing_ok=True)

<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: cd /Users/andycross/video-gen && uv run python << 'PYEOF'
import liblzfse, plistlib, struct

# Load Rotato-saved v6 (user fixed levitation + color)
raw_rotato = liblzfse.decompress(open('/tmp/drift-promo-v6-roundtrip.rotato', 'rb').read())

# Recreate what OUR pipeline produces from v5
raw_v5 = liblzfse.decompress(open('/tmp/drift-promo-v5.rotato', 'rb').read())
data_v5 = plistlib.loads(raw_v5)

import tempfile, subprocess
from pathlib import Path
with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(suffix='.plist', delete=False) as tmp:
    tmp_path = tmp.name
    plistlib.dump(data_v5, tmp, fmt=plistlib.FMT_BINARY)
subprocess.run(['plutil', '-convert', 'xml1', tmp_path], check=True, capture_output=True)
subprocess.run(['plutil', '-convert', 'binary1', tmp_path], check=True, capture_output=True)
raw_ours = Path(tmp_path).read_bytes()
Path(tmp_path).unlink()

# Find the float values for levitation in each
# In binary plist, 4-byte float: 0x22 followed by 4 bytes
# 8-byte double: 0x23 followed by 8 bytes

# Search for float encoding of 2.0
# IEEE 754 float32: 0x40000000
# IEEE 754 float64: 0x4000000000000000
f32_2 = b'\x22' + struct.pack('>f', 2.0)
f64_2 = b'\x23' + struct.pack('>d', 2.0)
f32_3 = b'\x22' + struct.pack('>f', 3.0)
f64_3 = b'\x23' + struct.pack('>d', 3.0)

print('=== Searching for float encodings ===')
for label, raw in [('Rotato-saved', raw_rotato), ('Our pipeline', raw_ours)]:
    print(f'\n{label} ({len(raw)} bytes):')
    for name, pattern in [('f32(2.0)', f32_2), ('f64(2.0)', f64_2), ('f32(3.0)', f32_3), ('f64(3.0)', f64_3)]:
        positions = []
        start = 0
        while True:
            pos = raw.find(pattern, start)
            if pos == -1: break
            positions.append(pos)
            start = pos + 1
        if positions:
            print(f'  {name}: found at {positions}')

# Look at the specific region around levitation
lev_str = b'levitation'
for label, raw in [('Rotato-saved', raw_rotato), ('Our pipeline', raw_ours)]:
    pos = raw.find(lev_str)
    print(f'\n{label} "levitation" at offset {pos}:')
    # Show 30 bytes after the end of "levitation"
    after = raw[pos+len(lev_str):pos+len(lev_str)+60]
    print(f'  after: {after.hex()}')
    # Annotate
    # Decode values
    i = 0
    vals = []
    data_region = after
    while i < len(data_region) and len(vals) < 10:
        b = data_region[i]
        if b & 0xF0 == 0x80:  # UID (1-byte)
            val = data_region[i+1] if i+1 < len(data_region) else '?'
            vals.append(f'UID({val})')
            i += 2
        elif b == 0x22:  # 4-byte real
            if i + 5 <= len(data_region):
                f = struct.unpack('>f', data_region[i+1:i+5])[0]
                vals.append(f'float32({f})')
            i += 5
        elif b == 0x23:  # 8-byte real
            if i + 9 <= len(data_region):
                d = struct.unpack('>d', data_region[i+1:i+9])[0]
                vals.append(f'float64({d})')
            i += 9
        elif b & 0xF0 == 0xD0:  # dict
            vals.append(f'dict({b & 0x0F} entries)')
            break
        elif b & 0xF0 == 0x10:  # int
            size = 1 << (b & 0x0F)
            vals.append(f'int{size*8}')
            i += 1 + size
        elif b == 0x09:
            vals.append('true')
            i += 1
        elif b == 0x08:
            vals.append('false')
            i += 1
        else:
            vals.append(f'byte(0x{b:02x})')
            i += 1
    print(f'  decoded: {vals}')
PYEOF

<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: cd /Users/andycross/video-gen && uv run python << 'PYEOF'
import liblzfse
import struct

raw = liblzfse.decompress(open('/tmp/drift-promo-v5.rotato', 'rb').read())

# Binary plist structure:
# Header: "bplist00" (8 bytes)
# Objects: sequential
# Offset table: array of offsets
# Trailer: last 32 bytes

assert raw[:8] == b'bplist00'

# Parse trailer (last 32 bytes)
trailer = raw[-32:]
(unused, offset_size, ref_size, num_objects, top_object, 
 offset_table_offset) = struct.unpack('>6xBBQQQ', trailer)

print(f'Offset entry size: {offset_size}')
print(f'Object ref size: {ref_size}')
print(f'Num objects: {num_objects}')
print(f'Top object: {top_object}')
print(f'Offset table at: {offset_table_offset}')
print(f'Total size: {len(raw)}')

# Read offset table
offsets = []
for i in range(num_objects):
    start = offset_table_offset + i * offset_size
    if offset_size == 1:
        offsets.append(raw[start])
    elif offset_size == 2:
        offsets.append(struct.unpack('>H', raw[start:start+2])[0])
    elif offset_size == 3:
        offsets.append(int.from_bytes(raw[start:start+3], 'big'))
    elif offset_size == 4:
        offsets.append(struct.unpack('>I', raw[start:start+4])[0])

# Find string objects containing our target text
targets = {
    'Navigate Your World': 'Every Street. Every Beat.',
    'AI-powered directions that actually make sense': 'Your AI-powered guide to the Feria',
}

def decode_string_at(data, offset):
    """Decode a binary plist string object. Returns (string, total_bytes)."""
    type_byte = data[offset]
    type_hi = type_byte & 0xF0
    
    if type_hi not in (0x50, 0x60):  # ASCII or UTF-16
        return None, 0
    
    is_utf16 = (type_hi == 0x60)
    length = type_byte & 0x0F
    pos = offset + 1
    
    if length == 0x0F:  # extended length
        int_type = data[pos]
        int_size = 1 << (int_type & 0x0F)
        pos += 1
        length = int.from_bytes(data[pos:pos+int_size], 'big')
        pos += int_size
    
    if is_utf16:
        s = data[pos:pos+length*2].decode('utf-16-be')
        return s, pos + length * 2 - offset
    else:
        s = data[pos:pos+length].decode('ascii')
        return s, pos + length - offset

def encode_ascii_string(s):
    """Encode a string as binary plist ASCII string."""
    b = s.encode('ascii')
    length = len(b)
    if length < 15:
        return bytes([0x50 | length]) + b
    else:
        # Extended length
        if length < 256:
            return bytes([0x5F, 0x10]) + bytes([length]) + b
        elif length < 65536:
            return bytes([0x5F, 0x11]) + struct.pack('>H', length) + b
        else:
            return bytes([0x5F, 0x12]) + struct.pack('>I', length) + b

# Find and plan replacements
replacements = []  # (obj_index, offset, old_size, new_bytes)

for obj_idx in range(num_objects):
    off = offsets[obj_idx]
    s, size = decode_string_at(raw, off)
    if s in targets:
        new_str = targets[s]
        new_bytes = encode_ascii_string(new_str)
        print(f'\nObject [{obj_idx}] at offset {off}: "{s}" ({size} bytes)')
        print(f'  -> "{new_str}" ({len(new_bytes)} bytes, delta: {len(new_bytes)-size})')
        replacements.append((obj_idx, off, size, new_bytes))

# Apply replacements (sort by offset descending so later offsets don't shift)
replacements.sort(key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)

result = bytearray(raw)
total_delta = 0

for obj_idx, off, old_size, new_bytes in replacements:
    delta = len(new_bytes) - old_size
    # Replace the bytes
    result[off:off+old_size] = new_bytes
    
    # Adjust offsets for all objects after this one
    for i in range(num_objects):
        if offsets[i] > off:
            offsets[i] += delta
    
    # Adjust offset_table_offset
    offset_table_offset += delta
    total_delta += delta

# Rebuild offset table
new_offset_table = bytearray()
for off in offsets:
    if offset_size == 1:
        new_offset_table.append(off)
    elif offset_size == 2:
        new_offset_table += struct.pack('>H', off)
    elif offset_size == 3:
        new_offset_table += off.to_bytes(3, 'big')
    elif offset_size == 4:
        new_offset_table += struct.pack('>I', off)

# Replace offset table in result
old_ot_start = len(raw) - 32 + total_delta - len(offsets) * offset_size  # wrong
# Actually, the offset table starts at offset_table_offset
result[offset_table_offset:offset_table_offset+len(new_offset_table)] = new_offset_table

# Update trailer
new_trailer = struct.pack('>6xBBQQQ', offset_size, ref_size, num_objects, top_object, offset_table_offset)
result[-32:] = new_trailer

```bash
cd /Users/andycross/video-gen && uv run python << 'PYEOF'
import liblzfse
import struct

raw = liblzfse.decompress(open('/tmp/drift-promo-v5.rotato', 'rb').read())

# Binary plist structure:
# Header: "bplist00" (8 bytes)
# Objects: sequential
# Offset table: array of offsets
# Trailer: last 32 bytes

assert raw[:8] == b'bplist00'

# Parse trailer (last 32 bytes)
trailer = raw[-32:]
(unused, offset_size, ref_size, num_objects, top_object, 
 offset_table_offset) = struct.unpack('>6xBBQQQ', trailer)

print(f'Offset entry size: {offset_size}')
print(f'Object ref size: {ref_size}')
print(f'Num objects: {num_objects}')
print(f'Top object: {top_object}')
print(f'Offset table at: {offset_table_offset}')
print(f'Total size: {len(raw)}')

# Read offset table
offsets = []
for i in range(num_objects):
    start = offset_table_offset + i * offset_size
    if offset_size == 1:
        offsets.append(raw[start])
    elif offset_size == 2:
        offsets.append(struct.unpack('>H', raw[start:start+2])[0])
    elif offset_size == 3:
        offsets.append(int.from_bytes(raw[start:start+3], 'big'))
    elif offset_size == 4:
        offsets.append(struct.unpack('>I', raw[start:start+4])[0])

# Find string objects containing our target text
targets = {
    'Navigate Your World': 'Every Street. Every Beat.',
    'AI-powered directions that actually make sense': 'Your AI-powered guide to the Feria',
}

def decode_string_at(data, offset):
    """Decode a binary plist string object. Returns (string, total_bytes)."""
    type_byte = data[offset]
    type_hi = type_byte & 0xF0
    
    if type_hi not in (0x50, 0x60):  # ASCII or UTF-16
        return None, 0
    
    is_utf16 = (type_hi == 0x60)
    length = type_byte & 0x0F
    pos = offset + 1
    
    if length == 0x0F:  # extended length
        int_type = data[pos]
        int_size = 1 << (int_type & 0x0F)
        pos += 1
        length = int.from_bytes(data[pos:pos+int_size], 'big')
        pos += int_size
    
    if is_utf16:
        s = data[pos:pos+length*2].decode('utf-16-be')
        return s, pos + length * 2 - offset
    else:
        s = data[pos:pos+length].decode('ascii')
        return s, pos + length - offset

def encode_ascii_string(s):
    """Encode a string as binary plist ASCII string."""
    b = s.encode('ascii')
    length = len(b)
    if length < 15:
        return bytes([0x50 | length]) + b
    else:
        # Extended length
        if length < 256:
            return bytes([0x5F, 0x10]) + bytes([length]) + b
        elif length < 65536:
            return bytes([0x5F, 0x11]) + struct.pack('>H', length) + b
        else:
            return bytes([0x5F, 0x12]) + struct.pack('>I', length) + b

# Find and plan replacements
replacements = []  # (obj_index, offset, old_size, new_bytes)

for obj_idx in range(num_objects):
    off = offsets[obj_idx]
    s, size = decode_string_at(raw, off)
    if s in targets:
        new_str = targets[s]
        new_bytes = encode_ascii_string(new_str)
        print(f'\nObject [{obj_idx}] at offset {off}: "{s}" ({size} bytes)')
        print(f'  -> "{new_str}" ({len(new_bytes)} bytes, delta: {len(new_bytes)-size})')
        replacements.append((obj_idx, off, size, new_bytes))

# Apply replacements (sort by offset descending so later offsets don't shift)
replacements.sort(key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)

result = bytearray(raw)
total_delta = 0

for obj_idx, off, old_size, new_bytes in replacements:
    delta = len(new_bytes) - old_size
    # Replace the bytes
    result[off:off+old_size] = new_bytes
    
    # Adjust offsets for all objects after this one
    for i in range(num_objects):
        if offsets[i] > off:
            offsets[i] += delta
    
    # Adjust offset_table_offset
    offset_table_offset += delta
    total_delta += delta

# Rebuild offset table
new_offset_table = bytearray()
for off in offsets:
    if offset_size == 1:
        new_offset_table.append(off)
    elif offset_size == 2:
        new_offset_table += struct.pack('>H', off)
    elif offset_size == 3:
        new_offset_table += off.to_bytes(3, 'big')
    elif offset_size == 4:
        new_offset_table += struct.pack('>I', off)

# Replace offset table in result
old_ot_start = len(raw) - 32 + total_delta - len(offsets) * offset_size  # wrong
# Actually, the offset table starts at offset_table_offset
result[offset_table_offset:offset_table_offset+len(new_offset_table)] = new_offset_table

# Update trailer
new_trailer = struct.pack('>6xBBQQQ', offset_size, ref_size, num_objects, top_object, offset_table_offset)
result[-32:] = new_trailer

# Verify
print(f'\nTotal delta: {total_delta} bytes')
print(f'Original: {len(raw)}, New: {len(result)}')

# Compress and save
compressed = liblzfse.compress(bytes(result))
open('/tmp/drift-promo-v8.rotato', 'wb').write(compressed)
print('Saved drift-promo-v8.rotato')

# Quick verify by searching for the new strings
for target in targets.values():
    pos = bytes(result).find(target.encode('ascii'))
    print(f'  Found "{target}" at offset {pos}')
```

    type_hi = type_byte & 0xF0
    if type_hi not in (0x50, 0x60):

    is_utf16 = (type_hi == 0x60)
    length = type_byte & 0x0F
    pos = offset + 1
    if length == 0x0F:
        int_type = data[pos]
        int_size = 1 << (int_type & 0x0F)
        pos += 1
        length = int.from_bytes(data[pos:pos+int_size], 'big')
        pos += int_size
    if is_utf16:
        s = data[pos:pos+length*2].decode('utf-16-be')
        return s, pos + length * 2 - offset
    else:
        s = data[pos:pos+length].decode('ascii')
        return s, pos + length - offset

def encode_ascii_string(s):
    b = s.encode('ascii')
    length = len(b)
    if length < 15:
        return bytes([0x50 | length]) + b
    elif length < 256:
        return bytes([0x5F, 0x10]) + bytes([length]) + b
    elif length < 65536:
        return bytes([0x5F, 0x11]) + struct.pack('>H', length) + b

targets = {
    'Navigate Your World': 'Every Street. Every Beat.',
    'AI-powered directions that actually make sense': 'Your AI-powered guide to the Feria',
}

        print(f'  -> "{new_str}" ({len(new_bytes)} bytes, delta: {len(new_bytes)-size})')
        replacements.append((obj_idx, off, size, new_bytes))

# Apply replacements from end to start (so earlier offsets don't shift)
replacements.sort(key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)

result = bytearray(raw)
cumulative_delta = 0

# Track offset adjustments
offset_adjustments = {}  # original_offset -> delta_to_apply

for obj_idx, off, old_size, new_bytes in replacements:
    delta = len(new_bytes) - old_size
    # Since we process from end to start, we can directly splice
    result[off:off+old_size] = new_bytes
    # Record: any offset > off needs adjustment by delta
    offset_adjustments[off] = delta

# Now rebuild: we need to recalculate all offsets
# Since we processed end-to-start, we need to compute total shifts
# For each object, how much did its offset shift?

for replace_off, delta in sorted(offset_adjustments.items()):

        if new_offsets[i] > replace_off:
            new_offsets[i] += delta

# Compute new offset table position

for replace_off, delta in sorted(offset_adjustments.items()):
    if offset_table_offset > replace_off:
        new_ot_offset += delta

# Check if offset_size is still sufficient
max_offset = max(new_offsets) if new_offsets else 0
print(f'\nMax offset: {max_offset}, offset_size: {offset_size}')

# Write new offset table
new_ot = bytearray()
for off in new_offsets:
    new_ot += off.to_bytes(offset_size, 'big')

# Splice in new offset table
result[new_ot_offset:new_ot_offset + len(new_ot)] = new_ot

# Update trailer
new_trailer = bytearray(32)
new_trailer[5] = sort_version
new_trailer[6] = offset_size
new_trailer[7] = ref_size
struct.pack_into('>Q', new_trailer, 8, num_objects)
struct.pack_into('>Q', new_trailer, 16, top_object)
struct.pack_into('>Q', new_trailer, 24, new_ot_offset)
result[-32:] = new_trailer

print(f'Total delta: {total_delta} bytes')

elif b == 0x23:

# Compress and save
compressed = liblzfse.compress(bytes(result))
open('/tmp/drift-promo-v8.rotato', 'wb').write(compressed)
print('\nSaved drift-promo-v8.rotato')

```bash
cd /Users/andycross/video-gen && uv run python << 'PYEOF'
```
import liblzfse, plistlib

# Load both files
raw_orig = liblzfse.decompress(open('/tmp/drift-promo-v9-copy.rotato', 'rb').read())
raw_resaved = liblzfse.decompress(open('/tmp/drift-promo-v9-copy-resaved.rotato', 'rb').read())

data_orig = plistlib.loads(raw_orig)
data_resaved = plistlib.loads(raw_resaved)

objs_orig = data_orig['$objects']
objs_resaved = data_resaved['$objects']

print(f'Original objects: {len(objs_orig)}')
print(f'Resaved objects: {len(objs_resaved)}')

# Find label stores in both
def find_label_store(objects):
    for i, obj in enumerate(objects):
        if isinstance(obj, dict) and 'levitation' in obj:
            return i, obj
    return None, None

idx_o, ls_o = find_label_store(objs_orig)
idx_r, ls_r = find_label_store(objs_resaved)

print(f'\n=== ORIGINAL label store [{idx_o}] ===')
for k, v in ls_o.items():
    if k == '$class': continue
    print(f'  {k}: {v!r} ({type(v).__name__})')

print(f'\n=== RESAVED label store [{idx_r}] ===')
for k, v in ls_r.items():
    if k == '$class': continue
    print(f'  {k}: {v!r} ({type(v).__name__})')

# Compare all objects to find differences
diffs = 0
for i in range(min(len(objs_orig), len(objs_resaved))):
    o = objs_orig[i]
    r = objs_resaved[i]
    if o != r:
        diffs += 1
        if diffs <= 20:
            if isinstance(o, dict) and isinstance(r, dict):
                # Find different keys
                all_keys = set(o.keys()) | set(r.keys())
                for k in all_keys:
                    ov = o.get(k)
                    rv = r.get(k)
                    if ov != rv:
                        if isinstance(ov, bytes) and len(ov) > 50:
                            ov = f'<bytes {len(ov)}>'
                        if isinstance(rv, bytes) and len(rv) > 50:
                            rv = f'<bytes {len(rv)}>'
                        print(f'\n  [{i}] key "{k}": {ov!r} -> {rv!r}')
            elif isinstance(o, str) and isinstance(r, str):
                if o != r:
                    print(f'\n  [{i}] str: "{o}" -> "{r}"')
            elif type(o) != type(r):
                ov = f'<bytes {len(o)}>' if isinstance(o, bytes) and len(o) > 50 else repr(o)
                rv = f'<bytes {len(r)}>' if isinstance(r, bytes) and len(r) > 50 else repr(r)
                print(f'\n  [{i}] type change: {ov} -> {rv}')
            else:
                ov = f'<bytes {len(o)}>' if isinstance(o, bytes) and len(o) > 50 else repr(o)
                rv = f'<bytes {len(r)}>' if isinstance(r, bytes) and len(r) > 50 else repr(r)
                print(f'\n  [{i}] value: {ov} -> {rv}')

if len(objs_resaved) > len(objs_orig):
    print(f'\n  +{len(objs_resaved) - len(objs_orig)} new objects added')

print(f'\nTotal different objects: {diffs}')
PYEOF

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Frame 3 is the best -- it shows the 3D map with the blue direction path, colorful casetas, and the "Aseos Municipales" with a navigation route. Very dynamic and visually rich. Frame 1 is also good with the event listings. Let me use frame 3 for the most visual impact.

Now let me build the whole thing. My plan:
1. Start from a fresh template copy
2. Inject the screen image (frame 3)
3. Create attributed strings with PyObjC for spicy marketing text
4. Set background, shadows, FOV, duration, render config


5. Use binary patching to preserve the float32 encoding

The levitation issue is a Rotato bug that never loads on reopen, but the plistlib+plutil round-trip works fine for text and basic properties. I'll start from the template, set the text with proper attributed strings, and use binary patching to preserve the exact binary format while making my changes.

Actually, binary patching only handles string replacement easily. Since I need to inject a new image, modify attributed strings, set background gradients, shadows, FOV, and duration—most of which are modifications to existing objects rather than structural changes—the binary approach gets complex fast.

Better approach: stick with plistlib to build everything, use the plutil round-trip for the final write, and accept that levitation won't work (it's a Rotato bug anyway). This should handle text, backgrounds, shadows, and everything else cleanly.

Let me just implement this end-to-end in one script.</details></think>

<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: cd /Users/andycross/video-gen && uv run python << 'PYEOF'
import Foundation
import AppKit
import plistlib
import shutil
from pathlib import Path
from rotato.io import read_plist, write_plist
from rotato.archive import KeyedArchive
from rotato import file as rf, modify

# === 1. FRESH TEMPLATE ===
output = '/tmp/drift-spicy.rotato'
shutil.copy2('templates/iphone14pro.rotato', output)

# === 2. INJECT SCREEN IMAGE ===
data = read_plist(output)
archive = KeyedArchive(data)
objects = data['$objects']

screen_idx, screen_node = archive.find_node_by_name('Screen')
geom = archive.resolve(screen_node['geometry'])
mats = archive.resolve(geom['materials'])
mat = archive.resolve(mats['NS.objects'][0])
diffuse = archive.resolve(mat['diffuse'])
img_data = open('/tmp/drift_frame_003.png', 'rb').read()
new_idx = len(objects)
objects.append(img_data)
diffuse['contents'] = plistlib.UID(new_idx)
print(f'Screen image injected at [{new_idx}] ({len(img_data):,} bytes)')

# === 3. ATTRIBUTED STRING LABELS ===
def make_attributed_string(text, font_name, font_size):
    astr = Foundation.NSMutableAttributedString.alloc().initWithString_(text)
    font = AppKit.NSFont.fontWithName_size_(font_name, font_size)
    if not font:
        raise ValueError(f"Font '{font_name}' not found")
    attrs = {AppKit.NSFontAttributeName: font}
    astr.setAttributes_range_(attrs, Foundation.NSMakeRange(0, astr.length()))
    archiver = Foundation.NSKeyedArchiver.alloc().initRequiringSecureCoding_(False)
    archiver.encodeObject_forKey_(astr, 'root')
    archiver.finishEncoding()
    return plistlib.loads(bytes(archiver.encodedData()))

def graft(target_objects, source_plist):
    src = source_plist['$objects']
    root_uid = source_plist['$top']['root']
    base = len(target_objects)
    def remap(val):
        if isinstance(val, plistlib.UID):
            return plistlib.UID(0) if val.data == 0 else plistlib.UID(val.data + base - 1)
        elif isinstance(val, dict):
            return {k: remap(v) for k, v in val.items()}
        elif isinstance(val, list):
            return [remap(v) for v in val]
        return val
    for i in range(1, len(src)):
        target_objects.append(remap(src[i]))
    return plistlib.UID(root_uid.data + base - 1)

headline_plist = make_attributed_string('Lost? Never.', 'HelveticaNeue-Bold', 380)
subtitle_plist = make_attributed_string('Your night out, mapped.', 'HelveticaNeue-Light', 220)

headline_uid = graft(objects, headline_plist)
subtitle_uid = graft(objects, subtitle_plist)

root = archive.root
label_store = archive.resolve(root['sceneLabelStore'])
all_dict = archive.resolve(label_store['all'])
keys = [archive.resolve(k) for k in all_dict['NS.keys']]

def set_label_color_rgb(color_obj, r, g, b):
    color_obj['NSColorSpace'] = 1
    color_obj['NSRGB'] = f'{r} {g} {b}\x00'.encode('ascii')
    color_obj['NSComponents'] = f'{r} {g} {b} 1'.encode('ascii')
    for old_key in ['NSWhite', 'NSCustomColorSpace']:
        if old_key in color_obj:
            del color_obj[old_key]

for i, k in enumerate(keys):
    label = archive.resolve(all_dict['NS.objects'][i])
    color = archive.resolve(label['color'])
    
    if k == 'sceneLabelTop':
        label['text'] = headline_uid
        set_label_color_rgb(color, 1, 1, 1)
        print(f'Headline -> sceneLabelTop')
    elif k == 'sceneLabelBottom':
        label['text'] = subtitle_uid
        set_label_color_rgb(color, 0.75, 0.85, 0.95)
        print(f'Subtitle -> sceneLabelBottom')
    else:
        text_obj = archive.resolve(label['text'])
        nsstring = archive.resolve(text_obj.get('NSString', plistlib.UID(0)))
        if isinstance(nsstring, dict) and 'NS.string' in nsstring:
            nsstring['NS.string'] = ''

label_store['scale'] = 1.0
label_store['levitation'] = 2.0  # Will need manual set (Rotato bug)

# === 4. STYLE & SETTINGS ===
# Deep orange-to-dark gradient (warm feria vibes)
bg = archive.resolve(root['sceneBackgroundContents'])
env = archive.resolve(root['environmentContents'])

for target in [bg, env]:
    bdata = archive.resolve(target)
    if isinstance(bdata, bytes):
        inner = plistlib.loads(bdata)
        inner['contentType'] = 'linear'
        inner['gradientStartColor'] = {'red': 0.95, 'green': 0.35, 'blue': 0.15}
        inner['gradientEndColor'] = {'red': 0.12, 'green': 0.05, 'blue': 0.15}
        idx = data['$objects'].index(bdata)
        data['$objects'][idx] = plistlib.dumps(inner, fmt=plistlib.FMT_BINARY)

# Shadows, glass, duration, FOV, render
root['sceneShadows'] = True
root['shadowIntensity'] = 0.7
root['showGlass'] = True
root['totalDuration'] = 5.0

# FOV via cameraConfig
cam_data = archive.resolve(root['cameraConfig'])
if isinstance(cam_data, bytes):
    cam = plistlib.loads(cam_data)
    cam['fieldOfView'] = 50.0
    idx = data['$objects'].index(cam_data)
    data['$objects'][idx] = plistlib.dumps(cam, fmt=plistlib.FMT_BINARY)

# Render config
render_data = archive.resolve(root['renderConfig'])
if isinstance(render_data, bytes):
    rc = plistlib.loads(render_data)
    rc['resolution'] = '2K'
    rc['fps'] = 60
    idx = data['$objects'].index(render_data)
    data['$objects'][idx] = plistlib.dumps(rc, fmt=plistlib.FMT_BINARY)

write_plist(data, output)
print(f'\nSaved {output}')
print('Note: set levitation manually after opening (Rotato bug)')
PYEOF
</summary>

import Foundation, AppKit, plistlib, json, shutil
from pathlib import Path
from rotato.io import read_plist, write_plist
from rotato.archive import KeyedArchive

output = '/tmp/drift-spicy.rotato'
shutil.copy2('templates/iphone14pro.rotato', output)

data = read_plist(output)
archive = KeyedArchive(data)
objects = data['$objects']
root = archive.root

# === SCREEN IMAGE ===
screen_idx, screen_node = archive.find_node_by_name('Screen')
geom = archive.resolve(screen_node['geometry'])
mats = archive.resolve(geom['materials'])
mat = archive.resolve(mats['NS.objects'][0])
diffuse = archive.resolve(mat['diffuse'])
img_bytes = open('/tmp/drift_frame_003.png', 'rb').read()
objects.append(img_bytes)
diffuse['contents'] = plistlib.UID(len(objects) - 1)
print(f'Screen: {len(img_bytes):,} bytes')

# === LABELS (PyObjC attributed strings) ===
def make_astr(text, font_name, font_size):
    a = Foundation.NSMutableAttributedString.alloc().initWithString_(text)
    f = AppKit.NSFont.fontWithName_size_(font_name, font_size)
    a.setAttributes_range_({AppKit.NSFontAttributeName: f}, Foundation.NSMakeRange(0, a.length()))
    arch = Foundation.NSKeyedArchiver.alloc().initRequiringSecureCoding_(False)
    arch.encodeObject_forKey_(a, 'root')
    arch.finishEncoding()
    return plistlib.loads(bytes(arch.encodedData()))

def graft(tgt, src_plist):
    src = src_plist['$objects']
    uid = src_plist['$top']['root']
    base = len(tgt)
    def remap(v):
        if isinstance(v, plistlib.UID):
            return plistlib.UID(0) if v.data == 0 else plistlib.UID(v.data + base - 1)
        elif isinstance(v, dict): return {k: remap(x) for k, x in v.items()}
        elif isinstance(v, list): return [remap(x) for x in v]
        return v
    for i in range(1, len(src)):
        tgt.append(remap(src[i]))
    return plistlib.UID(uid.data + base - 1)

h_uid = graft(objects, make_astr('Lost? Never.', 'HelveticaNeue-Bold', 380))
s_uid = graft(objects, make_astr('Your night out, mapped.', 'HelveticaNeue-Light', 220))

label_store = archive.resolve(root['sceneLabelStore'])
all_dict = archive.resolve(label_store['all'])
keys = [archive.resolve(k) for k in all_dict['NS.keys']]

for i, k in enumerate(keys):
    label = archive.resolve(all_dict['NS.objects'][i])
    color = archive.resolve(label['color'])
    if k == 'sceneLabelTop':
        label['text'] = h_uid
        color['NSColorSpace'] = 1
        color['NSRGB'] = b'1 1 1\x00'
        for old in ['NSWhite', 'NSCustomColorSpace', 'NSComponents']:
            color.pop(old, None)
        print('Headline set')
    elif k == 'sceneLabelBottom':
        label['text'] = s_uid
        color['NSColorSpace'] = 1
        color['NSRGB'] = b'0.8 0.88 0.95\x00'
        for old in ['NSWhite', 'NSCustomColorSpace', 'NSComponents']:
            color.pop(old, None)
        print('Subtitle set')
    else:
        text_obj = archive.resolve(label['text'])
        ns = archive.resolve(text_obj.get('NSString', plistlib.UID(0)))
        if isinstance(ns, dict) and 'NS.string' in ns:
            ns['NS.string'] = ''

label_store['scale'] = 1.0
label_store['levitation'] = 2.0

# === BACKGROUND (warm sunset gradient) ===
bg_ref = root['sceneBackgroundContents']
bg_bytes = archive.resolve(bg_ref)
bg_json = json.loads(bg_bytes.decode('utf-8'))
bg_json['gradientSettings']['start'] = {'red': 0.95, 'green': 0.25, 'blue': 0.1}
bg_json['gradientSettings']['end'] = {'red': 0.08, 'green': 0.02, 'blue': 0.18}
bg_json['gradientSettings']['noise'] = False
bg_json['gradientSettings']['isCubeMap'] = False
# Set active content type to gradient
if 'colorSettings' in bg_json:
    bg_json['colorSettings']['contentType'] = {'gradient': {}}
objects[bg_ref.data] = json.dumps(bg_json).encode('utf-8')

env_ref = root['environmentContents']
env_bytes = archive.resolve(env_ref)
env_json = json.loads(env_bytes.decode('utf-8'))
env_json['gradientSettings']['start'] = {'red': 0.95, 'green': 0.25, 'blue': 0.1}
env_json['gradientSettings']['end'] = {'red': 0.08, 'green': 0.02, 'blue': 0.18}
if 'colorSettings' in env_json:
    env_json['colorSettings']['contentType'] = {'gradient': {}}
objects[env_ref.data] = json.dumps(env_json).encode('utf-8')
print('Background: sunset gradient')

# === CAMERA ===
cam_ref = root['cameraConfig']
cam_bytes = archive.resolve(cam_ref)
cam_json = json.loads(cam_bytes.decode('utf-8'))
cam_json['fieldOfView'] = 50.0
cam_json['saturation'] = 1.1
cam_json['contrast'] = 0.05
objects[cam_ref.data] = json.dumps(cam_json).encode('utf-8')
print(f'Camera: FOV 50, saturation 1.1')

# === SCENE SETTINGS ===
root['sceneShadows'] = True
root['shadowIntensity'] = 0.7
root['showGlass'] = True
root['totalDuration'] = 5.0
print('Shadows, glass, 5s duration')

# === SAVE ===
write_plist(data, output)
print(f'\nSaved: {output}')
PYEOF
